key_points: In India, every state has a Legislative Assembly where people elect their representatives.
MLA stands for Member of Legislative Assembly; they are elected directly by the people.
A constituency is a specific area from which all the voters choose one representative.
The political party that wins more than half the seats in the state is called the majority party.
The party in majority forms the government, while all other parties form the opposition.
examples: MLA Election: People in a district voting for a candidate to represent them in the state capital.
Chief Minister: The leader of the majority party who is chosen to lead the state government.
The Governor: The person appointed by the Central Government to be the head of the state.
Public Meeting: A way for people to show their unhappiness or support for government decisions.
formulas: Winning Party = More than 50% of the total seats in the Assembly
exam_tips: Remember that an MLA can belong to a specific political party or be an 'Independent' candidate.
The Legislative Assembly is the place where all MLAs meet to discuss the problems of the state.
Note that the Chief Minister and other ministers have the power to make decisions and run the government.
Understand that the Governor ensures that the state government works according to the rules of the Constitution.
